original_hypothesis.title: Re-whitelisting a Market Resets lastRewardBlock, Permanently Deleting Pending Rewards
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When governance calls whiteListLendingMarket with _isWhiteListed = true, it unconditionally sets marketInfo[_market].lastRewardBlock = uint64(block.number). If a market had accumulated rewards but was temporarily delisted (or if governance just calls it again), resetting lastRewardBlock wipes out all pending reward accumulation for the un-updated period.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: State overwrite without prior settlement. Modifying the time-tracking cursor without processing the elapsed time destroys the accrued value.

proof_of_signal.pattern: marketInfo[_market].lastRewardBlock = uint64(block.number) without calling update_market first

preconditions: Governance calls whiteListLendingMarket for the market

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Fails to call update_market(_market) before resetting the reward cursor
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Should call update_market before changing whitelist status or resetting lastRewardBlock

analysis_questions: Can governance accidentally wipe out weeks of rewards by re-whitelisting?
Is there any way to recover the lost rewards?
validation_requirements: Accumulate rewards for a market without updating
Verify pending rewards are permanently lost

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Governance decides to re-whitelist a temporarily suspended market, or accidentally calls the whitelist function again. The lastRewardBlock is reset to the current block, instantly deleting all rewards that users earned during the un-updated period prior to the call.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Loss of earned CANTO rewards for liquidity providers
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Liquidity providers in the affected market

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests for re-whitelisting markets with pending rewards

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Visible as a jump in lastRewardBlock without corresponding accCantoPerShare increase

validation_result.impact_summary: Re-whitelisting a temporarily delisted market resets lastRewardBlock to the current block without settling accumulated rewards, permanently deleting all pending CANTO rewards earned by liquidity providers during the unsettled period.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The whiteListLendingMarket function in LendingLedger.sol unconditionally sets marketInfo[_market].lastRewardBlock = uint64(block.number) whenever _isWhiteListed is true. It does not call update_market before doing so. The require guard `require(lendingMarketWhitelist[_market] != _isWhiteListed, "No change")` prevents calling this on an already-whitelisted market, but does NOT prevent the delist-then-relist sequence. When a market is delisted and then re-listed, all rewards accumulated between the last update_market call and the delisting event are permanently lost because the lastRewardBlock cursor jumps forward past those blocks. The update_market loop uses lastRewardBlock as its starting point: `uint256 i = market.lastRewardBlock; while (i < block.number)`. Once lastRewardBlock is reset to a later block number, those earlier blocks can never be processed, and accCantoPerShare never accumulates the skipped rewards.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The defect is clearly present in the whiteListLendingMarket function. The lastRewardBlock is set to block.number on line where `marketInfo[_market].lastRewardBlock = uint64(block.number)` without any prior call to update_market. The update_market function starts its accumulation loop from `market.lastRewardBlock`, so resetting this value skips all unprocessed blocks. Additionally, update_market itself cannot be called while the market is delisted due to `require(lendingMarketWhitelist[_market], "Market not whitelisted")`, making it impossible to settle rewards after delisting but before re-listing.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: When _isWhiteListed is true, lastRewardBlock is unconditionally set to block.number without first calling update_market to settle pending rewards. This permanently destroys any rewards accumulated between the previous lastRewardBlock and the current block.

update_market starts from market.lastRewardBlock and iterates to block.number to accumulate rewards. Once lastRewardBlock is reset by whiteListLendingMarket, the skipped blocks can never be processed. Also, update_market requires the market to be whitelisted, so it cannot be called during the delisted period.

calling_sequence: 1. Market is whitelisted at block A: whiteListLendingMarket(_market, true, _hasGauge) → lastRewardBlock = A
2. Users deposit via sync_ledger which calls update_market, advancing lastRewardBlock to block B
3. Rewards accumulate from block B to block C but no sync_ledger/update_market is called
4. Governance delists at block C: whiteListLendingMarket(_market, false, false) → whitelist set to false
5. update_market cannot be called (requires whitelist=true, but it's now false)
6. Governance re-lists at block D: whiteListLendingMarket(_market, true, false) → lastRewardBlock = D
7. Rewards from blocks B to C are permanently lost; the loop in update_market will start from block D
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The delist-then-relist sequence is valid because the require guard only prevents setting the same value: `require(lendingMarketWhitelist[_market] != _isWhiteListed)`. Going true→false→true passes this check each time. On re-listing, `marketInfo[_market].lastRewardBlock = uint64(block.number)` resets the cursor without settling. The update_market function at line 76 starts its loop with `uint256 i = market.lastRewardBlock`, which means all blocks before the new lastRewardBlock are permanently skipped. The whitelist check at line 69 `require(lendingMarketWhitelist[_market])` prevents calling update_market during the delisted period, making it impossible to settle before re-listing.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment configuration affects this vulnerability. The defect is in the core logic of whiteListLendingMarket.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Scenario: Market M is whitelisted at block 1000, users deposit at block 1010 (lastRewardBlock = 1010), cantoPerBlock is set to 1e18, gauge weight is assigned. No further interactions occur until block 5000. Governance decides to temporarily suspend market M for review, calling whiteListLendingMarket(M, false, false) at block 5000. After review, governance re-whitelists at block 6000: whiteListLendingMarket(M, true, false). The lastRewardBlock jumps from 1010 to 6000, permanently erasing ~3990 blocks worth of CANTO rewards for all liquidity providers in market M. These rewards can never be recovered since update_market's while loop starts from lastRewardBlock (now 6000) and only processes forward.
validation_result.impact_assessment: All liquidity providers in the affected market permanently lose CANTO rewards accumulated during the unsettled period. With a cantoPerBlock of 1e18 and even modest gauge weight (e.g., 10% = 1e17), thousands of blocks of unclaimed rewards are destroyed. The magnitude depends on how long the market went without update_market being called before delisting. There is no recovery mechanism — the rewards are simply never calculated.
recommended_mitigations: Call update_market(_market) within whiteListLendingMarket before setting lastRewardBlock, but only when the market is currently whitelisted (lendingMarketWhitelist[_market] == true and _isWhiteListed == false or re-listing)
Alternatively, only set lastRewardBlock when it is currently 0 (first-time whitelisting), not on re-whitelisting
Add a separate governance function to settle a market's rewards before delisting, which bypasses the whitelist check for this specific purpose
Consider removing the unconditional lastRewardBlock reset and instead using: if (marketInfo[_market].lastRewardBlock == 0) { marketInfo[_market].lastRewardBlock = uint64(block.number); }
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy LendingLedger with GaugeController. 2. Whitelist a market with gauge at block N. 3. Set cantoPerBlock for current epoch. 4. Deposit tokens via LiquidityGauge (triggers sync_ledger, sets lastRewardBlock). 5. Advance blocks significantly. 6. Call whiteListLendingMarket(market, false, false) to delist. 7. Call whiteListLendingMarket(market, true, false) to re-list. 8. Verify marketInfo[market].lastRewardBlock equals current block.number, not the earlier value. 9. Call claim and verify rewards are zero for the period between the original deposit and delisting.
uncertainties: The exact impact magnitude depends on how frequently update_market is called in practice; if every user interaction triggers it, the window of unsettled rewards may be small
In the gauge-enabled path, there may be an additional key mismatch issue (marketInfo keyed by _market vs the resolved gauge address) that could interact with this defect
additional_testing_needed: Test the full delist-then-relist cycle with pending unsettled rewards to quantify reward loss
Test whether the first-time whitelisting correctly initializes lastRewardBlock (this is correct behavior, only the re-whitelisting is problematic)
Test interaction with the gauge address resolution in sync_ledger to see if key mismatches compound the issue
